localRating.rationale: Nine own requirements considered together: seven at target (completeness, all of clarity, consistency, observability, usability) and two at minimum (invalid counter-examples not shown; format evolution/versioning unspecified). The two gaps are real but contained documentation shortfalls, not failures of the spec's core contract, so the whole-set verdict holds at target.

factorRatings.rationale: All three clarity requirements meet target: RFC 2119 vocabulary with stated bounds, rules distinguishable from rationale, and an upfront glossary plus at-use definitions. One undefined reference ('model-content group') keeps it from outstanding.
No contradictions found; one term per concept and worked examples track their rules.

Conformance rules are observable/testable and linter-decided (target). Held from outstanding by the missing shown invalid counter-examples (that requirement rated minimum), but the binding question 'can conformance be decided' is answered yes.

Minimal core and structural (nesting/body) extension are well specified, but 'how it evolves' is largely absent — no file-versioning or forward-compatibility path, and unrecognized frontmatter keys are not framed as extension.
Concept-before-detail ordering, scannable schema blocks/tables, plain prose, and realistic examples; a few dense passages (Analyze, Requirement) keep it from outstanding.

aggregateRating.rationale: Leaf target: aggregate equals local rating. The format spec satisfies its completeness, clarity, consistency, verifiability, and usability bars; extensibility is the weakest factor at minimum.
